title: Girly Edition

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence showing Homer in some mishap
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated character moment but predictable

jokes.text: I want a job. - You've got it.
jokes.setup: Someone asking for employment
jokes.punchline: Instant hiring without any process
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist humor about workplace hiring

jokes.text: I quit. [Screams]
jokes.setup: Character dealing with flames and pain
jokes.punchline: Immediate resignation after getting hired
jokes.explanation: Good quick escalation from hire to quit, economical timing

jokes.text: Man, you'd think the quality would dip after 5,500 shows.
jokes.setup: Watching Itchy and Scratchy cartoon violence
jokes.punchline: Commenting on show longevity while implying quality hasn't changed
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on cartoon show longevity, self-aware humor

jokes.text: Don't trust mice. Cats are made of glass.
jokes.setup: Asked what kids learn from Itchy and Scratchy
jokes.punchline: Absurd life lessons from cartoon violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd interpretation of cartoon physics as life lessons, very quotable

jokes.text: That Mattel and Mars Bar Quick Energy Chocobot Hour?
jokes.setup: Need for educational programming
jokes.punchline: Obviously commercial show masquerading as education
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate-sponsored children's programming

jokes.text: It's the tightest three hours and 10 minutes on TV.
jokes.setup: Listing Krusty's show elements including nap time
jokes.punchline: Claiming a bloated show is 'tight'
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Krusty's delusion about his show quality

jokes.text: We could trim the hobo parade to a lean 20.
jokes.setup: Being asked to cut 10 minutes from his show
jokes.punchline: 20 minutes is considered 'lean' for a hobo parade
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept that 20 minutes for hobos is 'lean,' great character voice

jokes.text: Ach. She's a bonny pile.
jokes.setup: Willie looking at a pile of leaves
jokes.punchline: Scottish appreciation for leaf pile aesthetics
jokes.explanation: Good character voice, Willie's poetic appreciation of simple things

jokes.text: You owe me a quarter. I didn't crack my skull.
jokes.setup: Kids skateboarding with potential for injury
jokes.punchline: Betting on whether serious injury will occur
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about kids casually betting on injury, good character moment

jokes.text: It's been raped of its 'bonny-ness.'
jokes.setup: Willie's leaf pile has been destroyed
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic Scottish mourning for leaves
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Willie's poetic language about mundane destruction

jokes.text: And she'll make a fine piece of kindling.
jokes.setup: Willie taking the skateboard
jokes.punchline: Practical Scottish use for the 'stolen' board
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Willie's practical, no-nonsense attitude

jokes.text: Sorry. Willie can't hear ya.
jokes.setup: Kids knocking on Willie's door
jokes.punchline: Willie playing bagpipes too loudly to hear anything
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup with bagpipes as noise barrier

jokes.text: Today's top story- Little girl on cloud nine as dream comes true.
jokes.setup: Lisa being told she's selected as anchor
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately frames her own reaction as news
jokes.explanation: Smart character moment showing Lisa's news instincts and ego

jokes.text: I know. I already jumped to that conclusion.
jokes.setup: Principal telling Lisa she's been selected
jokes.punchline: Lisa matter-of-factly admits she assumed the obvious
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intelligence and slight arrogance

jokes.text: Tell me who I selected to be lunchroom monitor. - Me?
jokes.setup: Principal testing Lisa's assumption skills
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately assumes she got another position too
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's pattern of assuming she gets every good position

jokes.text: I'll have to eat my way out. [Chomping]
jokes.setup: Willie trapped under pile of leaves
jokes.punchline: Treating leaves like edible escape route
jokes.explanation: Absurd solution to being buried in leaves, good physical comedy

jokes.text: My shack! My beautiful shack!
jokes.setup: Willie's shack has exploded
jokes.punchline: Calling a crude shack 'beautiful'
jokes.explanation: Character comedy showing Willie's attachment to his humble dwelling

jokes.text: The man knows quality work.
jokes.setup: Willie accusing Bart of destroying his shack
jokes.punchline: Bart takes Willie's accusation as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - turning destruction into a compliment on his skills

jokes.text: All your hard work and grade-grubbing have finally paid off.
jokes.setup: Marge praising Lisa's achievement
jokes.punchline: Calling Lisa's academic effort 'grade-grubbing'
jokes.explanation: Marge's backhanded compliment about Lisa's academic dedication

jokes.text: Well, we used to date. Plus, he threatened me.
jokes.setup: Asked why Nelson is on her news team
jokes.punchline: Romantic history plus intimidation as qualifications
jokes.explanation: Good character moment combining Lisa's past relationship with Nelson's bullying nature

jokes.text: That 'Mom' stuff doesn't work on me.
jokes.setup: Kids saying 'Mom' to get her attention
jokes.punchline: Marge claiming immunity to her children's basic communication
jokes.explanation: Relatable parenting humor about selective hearing

jokes.text: I got him after a robber shot me six times and left me for dead.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he has a helper monkey
jokes.punchline: Extreme violence described matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Apu's characteristic calm tone

jokes.text: Hey, I'm on TV. Fart.
jokes.setup: Bart realizing he's being filmed
jokes.punchline: Immediate lowbrow humor for the camera
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart behavior - immediately going for cheap laughs on camera

jokes.text: So we meet again, Mr. Bond. - Silence, Octopussy.
jokes.setup: Bart doing James Bond villain voice
jokes.punchline: Lisa playing along but still maintaining authority
jokes.explanation: Sibling dynamic with pop culture references, Lisa maintaining control

jokes.text: Where the hell's my grilled cheese?
jokes.setup: Kent giving encouraging words to kids
jokes.punchline: Immediately demanding food with profanity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kent's self-centeredness and professionalism dropping

jokes.text: Do they do any other tricks? - No. - Just the monkey then.
jokes.setup: Asking about seeing-eye dogs' capabilities
jokes.punchline: Homer only interested if they do tricks, not help blind people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer selfishness - missing the point of service animals

jokes.text: I'm not handicapped. I'm just lazy.
jokes.setup: Asked how he's differently-abled
jokes.punchline: Homer's honest admission of laziness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - perfect economy and character integration, completely honest about his flaws

jokes.text: Enfeebled? Ooh, I know just the guy.
jokes.setup: Told helper animals are for the enfeebled
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately thinking of exploiting someone else
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's scheming nature and willingness to exploit others

jokes.text: Mmm, I can't wait to eat that monkey.
jokes.setup: Homer driving away with helper monkey
jokes.punchline: Revealing he plans to eat the monkey
jokes.explanation: Dark turn that's perfectly in character for Homer's food obsession

jokes.text: First on the chopping block- periodicals. - Boring.
jokes.setup: Lisa reporting on library budget cuts
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive reaction to educational news
jokes.explanation: Simple character contrast - Lisa's earnestness vs Bart's dismissal

jokes.text: Lock the doggy in the barn 'cause here comes dodgeball action.
jokes.setup: Bart doing sports introduction
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical folksy saying that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Bart trying to sound like a real sportscaster with made-up colorful language

jokes.text: Disqualification for use of dog-doo on a stick.
jokes.setup: Reporting on schoolyard fight
jokes.punchline: Treating crude playground weapon as legitimate sports infraction
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination of formal sports reporting with playground grossness

jokes.text: 'Zazz.' - What is 'zazz'? - 'Zing,' 'zork,' 'kapowza'
jokes.setup: Executive explaining why Bart is good for TV
jokes.punchline: Making up nonsense words to describe charisma
jokes.explanation: Satirizing TV executive speak with meaningless invented terms

jokes.text: Bart's not exactly the brightest penny in the fountain.
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Bart's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor combining 'brightest bulb' with 'penny in the fountain'
jokes.explanation: Lisa's attempt at clever insult results in confused metaphor

jokes.text: In English, Lisa.
jokes.setup: Lisa speaking clearly about Bart's limitations
jokes.punchline: Executive acting like Lisa was speaking too intellectually
jokes.explanation: Executive's anti-intellectual bias, treating normal speech as too complex

jokes.text: Between you and me, he's, well... kind of dumb. [Whimpers]
jokes.setup: Lisa privately criticizing Bart's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Bart overhearing and reacting with hurt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing sibling dynamics and Bart's vulnerability

jokes.text: Monkey. There's a monkey in the house. - Relax. It's only Mojo.
jokes.setup: Marge panicking about monkey in house
jokes.punchline: Homer treating exotic pet as normal household addition
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpson family dynamic - Marge's reasonable concern vs Homer's casualness

jokes.text: This 'filthy monkey' made the orange juice you're drinking.
jokes.setup: Marge calling the monkey filthy
jokes.punchline: Revealing the unsanitary monkey prepared their food
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with practical implications

jokes.text: I won't turn to the comics. All right. I'll read one comic just to get me rolling. Archie.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to read serious news
jokes.punchline: Immediately giving in to comics, choosing most wholesome one
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's lack of willpower and choosing kiddie comic

jokes.text: 'Supreme Court reverses'- [Groans] I won't turn to the comics.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to read serious news
jokes.punchline: Struggling with complex topics, resisting comics
jokes.explanation: Relatable struggle with difficult reading material

jokes.text: Honey, I'd love to reassure you... but right now Mommy needs a tetanus shot.
jokes.setup: Bart asking if he's dumb
jokes.punchline: Marge too distracted by monkey-related injury to comfort him
jokes.explanation: Good timing - Bart's vulnerable moment interrupted by practical emergency

jokes.text: Sister, huh? [Chuckles] I've got a sister. Miss Big Shot CNN Washington Correspondent.
jokes.setup: Bart mentioning sibling rivalry
jokes.punchline: Kent relating with his own professional jealousy
jokes.explanation: Kent's petty jealousy of more successful sister

jokes.text: This is the most prestigious award that Del Monte gives.
jokes.setup: Kent pointing to another award
jokes.punchline: Prestigious journalism award from a canned food company
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate sponsorship of journalism awards

jokes.text: It's the sound of children's laughter... silenced.
jokes.setup: Kent demonstrating human interest story technique
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of quiet carousel
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of manipulative news emotion - melodramatic description of simple quiet

jokes.text: A store that sells designer mouse pads.
jokes.setup: Carousel being torn down for 'the future'
jokes.punchline: The 'future' is something trivial and already outdated
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire - 'progress' as silly 90s tech accessories, great prescient humor

jokes.text: The monkey's on my part of the sofa. - Honey, he's clearly marked his territory.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about monkey's location
jokes.punchline: Marge pointing out the monkey has urinated to claim space
jokes.explanation: Animal behavior humor with territory marking implications

jokes.text: Some say the ducks went to Canada. Others say Toronto.
jokes.setup: Bart reporting on missing ducks with authority
jokes.punchline: Treating Toronto as separate from Canada
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Bart's pseudo-journalism - confidently stating geographic nonsense

jokes.text: Smithers- [Sniffles] You think maybe my power plant killed those ducks? - There's no maybe about it, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns emotionally asking if he killed ducks
jokes.punchline: Smithers confirming it matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Burns' rare emotion met with Smithers' brutal honesty

jokes.text: [Sniffles] Excellent.
jokes.setup: Learning he definitely killed ducks
jokes.punchline: Burns pleased about environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns - going from emotional to evil in one word, perfect character consistency

jokes.text: That wasn't news. That was sappy, manipulative drivel.
jokes.setup: Lisa watching Bart's emotional duck story
jokes.punchline: Lisa correctly identifying manipulative journalism
jokes.explanation: Lisa's analytical nature cutting through emotional manipulation

jokes.text: This is where the tears would be if I could cry. But I can't. Botched face-lift.
jokes.setup: Kent pointing to his eyes
jokes.punchline: Can't cry due to cosmetic surgery gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Dark, unexpected revelation about Kent's vanity having serious consequences

jokes.text: It's Lisa. - Maryann's better.
jokes.setup: Kent calling Lisa by wrong name
jokes.punchline: Insisting his mistake is an improvement
jokes.explanation: Kent's dismissive attitude and refusal to be corrected

jokes.text: They'll be blowing their nose with a pancake.
jokes.setup: Lisa planning to pump audience full of sap
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of extreme emotional manipulation
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd image of crying so much you need breakfast food as tissue

jokes.text: Can anyone who loves animals that much really be crazy? [Screaming Gibberish]
jokes.setup: Lisa rhetorically asking if cat lady can be crazy
jokes.punchline: Immediate evidence of obvious insanity
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - rhetorical question immediately answered by evidence

jokes.text: Stop that. You're a helper monkey. This isn't helping.
jokes.setup: Monkey acting up
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of 'helper' role
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple logic about what constitutes 'helping'

jokes.text: Some purists will tell you the American flag doesn't contain the word 'Jordache.'
jokes.setup: Bart describing veterans' homemade flags
jokes.punchline: Flag containing designer jeans brand name
jokes.explanation: Absurd combination of patriotism with random fashion branding

jokes.text: That's what Veterans Day is for, Bart. - But is that really enough?
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out existing veterans holiday
jokes.punchline: Bart continuing to milk the emotional angle
jokes.explanation: Bart persisting with fake sentiment despite logical correction

jokes.text: They have two. - Well, maybe they should have three.
jokes.setup: Lisa correcting that veterans have two holidays
jokes.punchline: Bart arbitrarily suggesting more holidays
jokes.explanation: Bart's escalation showing he has no real conviction, just going with flow

jokes.text: Well, maybe they should have three. I'm Bart Simpson.
jokes.setup: Lisa pointing out veterans already have two holidays
jokes.punchline: Bart's weak response and signature sign-off
jokes.explanation: Bart's inability to defend his position and retreat to his catchphrase

jokes.text: If I'm guilty of anything, maybe it's caring too much.
jokes.setup: Lisa accusing Bart of not caring about people
jokes.punchline: Classic non-apology claiming excessive virtue as flaw
jokes.explanation: Classic deflection line that's become a comedy trope, well-executed here

jokes.text: His cholesterol's through the roof.
jokes.setup: Monkey appearing unhealthy
jokes.punchline: Helper monkey has human health problems from poor diet
jokes.explanation: Absurd consequence of monkey adopting human lifestyle

jokes.text: [Computerized Voice] Pray for Mojo.
jokes.setup: Monkey in rehabilitation center
jokes.punchline: Monkey using computerized voice to request prayers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd moment - monkey with Stephen Hawking-style voice asking for prayers, highly quotable

jokes.text: [Nelson imitating farts]
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Nelson to cover for technical difficulties
jokes.punchline: Nelson's idea of covering is making fart noises
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson behavior - defaulting to crude humor in crisis

jokes.text: Oh, Bart. It's me, a jolly policeman. Come out so we can give you a medal.
jokes.setup: Willie trying to lure Bart out of hiding
jokes.punchline: Transparent attempt at deception with obvious fake voice
jokes.explanation: Willie's obviously fake attempt to trick Bart, good character moment

jokes.text: You can't create a monster and then whine when he stomps on a few buildings.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending Bart to Willie
jokes.punchline: Using monster movie metaphor for parenting/society responsibility
jokes.explanation: Smart metaphor about societal responsibility using kaiju movie imagery, quotable

jokes.text: You're right. It's all Willie's fault. I've been a terrible father.
jokes.setup: Lisa's speech about societal responsibility
jokes.punchline: Willie completely misunderstanding and taking personal blame
jokes.explanation: Willie's complete misinterpretation of Lisa's sociological point as personal failing

jokes.text: Major Nougat, Gooey, Cocoa... put down those entertaining Mattel products.
jokes.setup: Children's action show with candy-themed characters
jokes.punchline: Blatant product placement disguised as dialogue
jokes.explanation: Satirizing blatant corporate marketing disguised as children's entertainment

tmdb_overview: Lisa becomes the anchor of a kids’ news program but is upstaged by Bart’s treacly human interest stories. At home, Homer corrupts a helper monkey.
insight: Solid joke volume masks Season 9's decline: 3.24 jokes per minute can't sustain narrative momentum.
